title: Chapter 10 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of networking and JSON!

questions.question: Which package is commonly used for HTTP requests in Flutter?
questions.explanation: The http package is the most common choice.

questions.question: jsonDecode returns a String.
questions.explanation: It returns a Map or List depending on the JSON.

questions.question: To use await, a function must be marked _____.
questions.explanation: async enables the use of await.

questions.question: What does the 'await' keyword do?
options: Pauses the function until the Future completes
Runs the Future in the background
Converts a Future to a value
Throws an error if the Future fails
questions.explanation: await suspends execution until the Future completes.

questions.question: A FutureBuilder rebuilds whenever its future completes.
questions.explanation: Yes, it rebuilds based on the latest snapshot.

questions.question: Which status code indicates success?
questions.explanation: 200 OK means the request succeeded.

questions.question: To catch a timeout, catch a _____ exception.
questions.explanation: TimeoutException is thrown when a timeout occurs.

questions.question: The 'http' package is part of the Flutter SDK.
questions.explanation: It's a separate package that must be added to pubspec.yaml.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a model class in networking?
options: To parse JSON into typed objects
questions.explanation: Model classes provide a type-safe representation of JSON data.

questions.question: To create a Uri from a string, use _____.
questions.explanation: Uri.parse is the standard way.

questions.question: FutureBuilder's builder is called multiple times as the future progresses.
questions.explanation: It's called initially, during waiting, and after completion.

questions.question: Which property of AsyncSnapshot indicates an error occurred?
questions.explanation: hasError is true when an error occurred.

questions.question: To convert a Dart object to JSON, use _____.
questions.explanation: jsonEncode serializes an object to a JSON string.

questions.question: You can only use FutureBuilder with Future<List<T>>.
questions.explanation: FutureBuilder works with any Future<T>.

questions.question: What does ConnectionState.done mean?
questions.explanation: done means the asynchronous operation has finished.

questions.question: To handle a missing internet connection, catch a _____ exception.
questions.explanation: SocketException is thrown for network-level issues.

questions.question: The 'async' keyword makes a function return a Future automatically.
questions.explanation: Yes, async functions always return a Future.

questions.question: Which method is used to add a timeout to a Future?
questions.explanation: Future.timeout() adds a time limit.

questions.question: In a model class, the factory constructor that creates an object from JSON is usually named _____.
questions.explanation: fromJson is the conventional name.

questions.question: JSONPlaceholder requires an API key.
questions.explanation: It's a free fake API with no authentication.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'http' package's 'read' method?
options: To make a GET request and return the body as a String
questions.explanation: http.read() is a convenience for GET and returns the response body.

questions.question: To send JSON data in a POST request, set the _____ header to 'application/json'.
questions.explanation: Content-Type tells the server the format of the request body.

questions.question: A Future can complete with an error.
questions.explanation: Yes, Futures can complete with either a value or an error.

questions.question: Which widget would you use to show a loading indicator while waiting for a Future?
questions.explanation: Both circular and linear progress indicators can be used.

questions.question: To parse a JSON array, you use jsonDecode and cast the result to _____.
questions.explanation: jsonDecode returns List<dynamic> for arrays.
